cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.19)
project(liqwm CXX C)
# C is needed ONLY for xdg-shell-protocol.c below -- that file is C code
# generated by wayland-scanner from the protocol XML. Without "C" here,
# CMake never sets up a C compiler for this project at all, and
# add_library() on a .c source fails with "Cannot determine link
# language for target" -- it's not that the language is ambiguous, it's
# that no C toolchain was ever registered to ask.

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 23)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)

find_package(PkgConfig REQUIRED)

# Aquamarine: Hyprland's backend/rendering abstraction library.
# Provides DRM/output/input device handling so we don't reimplement KMS, libinput, etc.
pkg_check_modules(AQUAMARINE REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET aquamarine)

pkg_check_modules(WAYLAND_SERVER REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET wayland-server)
pkg_check_modules(PIXMAN REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET pixman-1)
pkg_check_modules(HYPRUTILS REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET hyprutils)

# Aquamarine's own public headers (e.g. allocator/Allocator.hpp) #include
# <drm_fourcc.h> directly -- that's libdrm, not Aquamarine itself. Listed
# explicitly here rather than relying on it arriving transitively through
# aquamarine's .pc file, since that's exactly the assumption that broke:
# anyone building this needs `libdrm` installed as its own system package
# (Arch: `sudo pacman -S libdrm`; Debian/Ubuntu: `sudo apt install libdrm-dev`).
pkg_check_modules(LIBDRM REQUIRED IMPORTED_TARGET libdrm)

# --- xdg-shell protocol code generation ---
# wl_compositor/wl_surface come from libwayland itself (no generation
# needed, just wayland-server-protocol.h). xdg-shell is a SEPARATE
# protocol shipped as XML by the wayland-protocols package; wayland-scanner
# turns that XML into the C dispatch-table types/event-senders we use in
# src/wayland/XdgShell.cpp. This has to happen at build time on whatever
# machine you're building on -- the generated files aren't checked in.
find_program(WAYLAND_SCANNER_EXECUTABLE NAMES wayland-scanner)
if(NOT WAYLAND_SCANNER_EXECUTABLE)
    message(FATAL_ERROR "wayland-scanner not found -- install libwayland-bin (Debian/Ubuntu) or wayland (Arch)")
endif()

pkg_check_modules(WAYLAND_PROTOCOLS REQUIRED wayland-protocols)
pkg_get_variable(WAYLAND_PROTOCOLS_DIR wayland-protocols pkgdatadir)

set(XDG_SHELL_XML "${WAYLAND_PROTOCOLS_DIR}/stable/xdg-shell/xdg-shell.xml")
set(GENERATED_DIR "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/generated")
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY "${GENERATED_DIR}")

add_custom_command(
    OUTPUT "${GENERATED_DIR}/xdg-shell-protocol.h"
    COMMAND "${WAYLAND_SCANNER_EXECUTABLE}" server-header "${XDG_SHELL_XML}" "${GENERATED_DIR}/xdg-shell-protocol.h"
    DEPENDS "${XDG_SHELL_XML}"
    COMMENT "Generating xdg-shell-protocol.h"
)
add_custom_command(
    OUTPUT "${GENERATED_DIR}/xdg-shell-protocol.c"
    COMMAND "${WAYLAND_SCANNER_EXECUTABLE}" private-code "${XDG_SHELL_XML}" "${GENERATED_DIR}/xdg-shell-protocol.c"
    DEPENDS "${XDG_SHELL_XML}"
    COMMENT "Generating xdg-shell-protocol.c"
)

add_library(xdg-shell-protocol STATIC
    "${GENERATED_DIR}/xdg-shell-protocol.c"
    "${GENERATED_DIR}/xdg-shell-protocol.h"
)
# Both generated files are listed as sources here (the .h won't be
# compiled, .h files never are -- but listing it makes this target
# actually DEPEND on the custom command that generates it). Without this,
# nothing in the dependency graph ever requests xdg-shell-protocol.h, so
# wayland-scanner's "generate the header" step silently never runs, even
# though the .c-generating step does (because the .c file IS a real
# compiled source above). #include <xdg-shell-protocol.h> in C++ source
# files has no effect on CMake's build graph -- CMake doesn't parse
# #include directives to infer custom-command dependencies.
target_include_directories(xdg-shell-protocol PUBLIC "${GENERATED_DIR}")
target_link_libraries(xdg-shell-protocol PUBLIC PkgConfig::WAYLAND_SERVER)
# Generated C linked into a C++ project -- needs C++ linkage rules applied
# to its symbols' usage sites, not to the file itself, so no extra flags
# needed here; CMake mixed C/C++ linking handles this automatically as
# long as the final link step uses the C++ driver (it does, since the
# project language is CXX).

add_executable(liqwm
    src/main.cpp
    src/Compositor.cpp
    src/Camera.cpp
    src/Island.cpp
    src/layout/HyprMode.cpp
    src/layout/CanvasMode.cpp
    src/input/InputManager.cpp
    src/wayland/Surface.cpp
    src/wayland/XdgShell.cpp
    src/wayland/DataDevice.cpp
    src/wayland/Seat.cpp
    src/wayland/Output.cpp
)

target_include_directories(liqwm PRIVATE src)

target_link_libraries(liqwm PRIVATE
    PkgConfig::AQUAMARINE
    PkgConfig::WAYLAND_SERVER
    PkgConfig::PIXMAN
    PkgConfig::HYPRUTILS
    PkgConfig::LIBDRM
    xdg-shell-protocol
)

target_compile_options(liqwm PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter)
